understanding.goal_understanding: The goal is to navigate a tile grid and reach the goal gem tile marked `+`. Each level requires finding a path through the grid by moving horizontally and using gravity to fall to lower positions. The level is won when the player lands on or reaches the `+` tile.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement is horizontal only (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT), and after each horizontal move, the player falls in the direction of gravity (DOWN = toward higher y values) until landing on a solid tile. CLICK removes breakable blocks (x tiles) to open fall paths, and importantly CLICK actions do NOT consume movement-related resources in the same way moves do. The corridor scan and left_landing/right_landing previews show where the player will end up after gravity acts. Spikes (u/v tiles) instantly end the run if landed on. UNDO reverts the previous action for recovery. In this level, I was systematically clicking breakable blocks to create downward passages and moving left/right to navigate through different sections of the level, gradually ascending (since gravity was DOWN toward higher y, and I was moving to lower y values = visually upward). The episode ended at turn 14 with 50 steps remaining, suggesting the attempt timed out or the level structure ran out of turns from the episode perspective rather than the in-game steps.
